summary: The test suite covers the core requirements well: glob pattern conversion, matching, multi-label rules, priority ordering, deduplication, error validation, and an end-to-end CLI integration test. ConvertTo-GlobRegex and Test-GlobMatch have dedicated unit suites, Get-PrLabels is tested across a good range of scenarios, and Read-Rules has both success and failure cases. Notable gaps: Read-ChangedFiles has no tests at all (missing-file error, blank-line filtering); the JSON output format (-OutputFormat Json) is never exercised; the ? wildcard is tested only in regex conversion, not in actual Test-GlobMatch matching; same-label-across-multiple-rules priority resolution (min wins) is untested; and an empty rules array is not covered. Design is clean—BeforeAll dot-sourcing, descriptive names, proper finally-block cleanup of temp files, and appropriate Sort-Object use where order doesn't matter. The Should -Throw wildcard patterns are idiomatic. The main structural issue is that workflow-structure assertions live in Run-ActTests.ps1 (a separate harness) rather than in the Pester file, so Invoke-Pester alone does not validate the CI pipeline. The suite would catch most functional regressions but would miss a breakage in the JSON path or the file-reader error branch.
